segments.text: Welcome to the cockpit, data hunters. Overpass Turbo is live. Calm canvas now, storm next.
segments.fallback_text: Open Overpass Turbo and load the workspace.
segments.subtitle: Step 1: Open Overpass Turbo and load the map + query editor workspace

segments.text: Map nodes take a second. More math than high school. Boom, rendered.
segments.subtitle: Map nodes take a second. More math than high school. Boom, rendered.

segments.text: Split view. Query wand on the left, map battlefield on the right.
segments.fallback_text: Split view: editor left, map right.
segments.subtitle: Step 2: Split view: query editor on left, map + controls on right

segments.text: Hospitals in Hyderabad. Fixed city box, full Overpass QL, nodes, ways, relations. Feed the machine.
segments.subtitle: Step 3: Write a Hyderabad hospitals query in Overpass QL using a fixed city bounding box

segments.text: Run it. The API is sprinting across the planet to fetch every hospital marker it can find.
segments.subtitle: Step 4: Run query to load all mapped hospitals as explorable markers

segments.text: The API is hauling hospital markers worldwide. Fast fiber beats virtue.
segments.subtitle: The API is hauling hospital markers worldwide. Fast fiber beats virtue.

segments.text: Blue circles everywhere. Zoom to data and teleport onto Hyderabad.
segments.subtitle: Step 5: Use 'zoom to data' to jump map viewport directly onto Hyderabad results

segments.text: Click a marker and read the tags. Name, address, amenity type. Pure urban DNA.
segments.subtitle: Step 6: Click map markers to inspect feature tags like name, address, and amenity type

segments.text: Hit the Data tab. Raw OSM attributes. Nerd heaven.
segments.subtitle: Step 7: Open Data tab to inspect raw tabular output and OSM attributes

segments.text: Back to Map view so we can compare actual coverage.
segments.subtitle: Step 8: Switch back to Map view to visually compare coverage across neighborhoods

segments.text: Pivot time. Swap hospitals for EV chargers. Same city, totally different future.
segments.subtitle: Step 9: Replace with EV charging station query to analyze clean-mobility readiness

segments.text: Processing again. Come on, little data packets, do your thing.
segments.subtitle: Processing again. Come on, little data packets, do your thing.

segments.text: Rendering complete. See those gaps? That is urban planning screaming for attention.
segments.subtitle: Step 10: EV charging points rendered; now compare spread versus healthcare locations

segments.text: That spread tells you exactly where the future still needs building.
segments.subtitle: That spread tells you exactly where the future still needs building.

segments.text: Now add schools. Education layer, same boundary. Watch the city grow in fast-forward.
segments.subtitle: Step 11: Run school query to map education infrastructure in the same city boundary

segments.text: Schools are loading. Keep the processors humming. City timelapse, live.
segments.subtitle: Schools are loading. Keep the processors humming. City timelapse, live.

segments.text: One more. Add pharmacies and build a four-layer dataset cake. City hall wishes it had this.
segments.fallback_text: Add pharmacies as the fourth layer.
segments.subtitle: Step 12: Add pharmacy query as a fourth dataset to build a richer city-services comparison

segments.text: Final crunch. If the fan is spinning, that is just the sound of progress.
segments.fallback_text: Map is crunching the final layer.
segments.subtitle: Final crunch. If the fan is spinning, that is just the sound of progress.

segments.text: Single editor workflow. That is the superpower: iterate faster, analyze deeper, conquer the urban jungle.
segments.subtitle: Step 13: Single editor workflow lets you iterate quickly across multiple urban datasets

segments.text: One last pass. Look at that density. Pure data art.
segments.subtitle: One last pass. Look at that density. Pure data art.

segments.text: Open Export. We are done playing; now we go pro.
segments.subtitle: Step 14: Open Export options for downstream GIS and analytics workflows

segments.text: GeoJSON, KML, GPX, raw OSM. Pick your ammo and ship the data.
segments.subtitle: Step 15: Export supports GeoJSON, KML, GPX, and raw OSM for different tools

segments.text: Tutorial complete. Blank screen to geo-intelligence. Go bend maps to your will.
segments.subtitle: Step 16: Tutorial complete: from iterative query editing to exportable, reproducible geo-data
